# Quick start : OpenFOAM case using a mesh generated by Gmsh

1. copy the mesh file generated by Gmsh (*.msh) into this directory.
2. convert the mesh to OpenFOAM format
   ``` bash
   gmshToFoam foo.msh
   ```
3. check mesh quality
   ``` bash
   checkMesh
   ```
4. transform the mesh scale (from mm to m) <br>

   If the mesh was created in millimeters, convert it to meters:
   ``` bash
   transformPoints -scale "(1e-3 1e-3 1e-3)"
   ```
5. Run the steady-state simulation
   ``` bash
   simpleFoam | tee log
   ```
6. After the simulation, compute wall shear stress (WSS) and convert the results:
   ``` bash
   simpleFoam -postProcess -func "wallShearStress(patches (WALL); writeFields yes;)" -latestTime
   python pa_convert.py --rho 1060 --time latest
   ```
# Run Parallel
``` bash
chmod +x simpleParallel.sh
./simpleParallel
```

# 注意点
並列実行を行うときは`simpleParallel.sh`中で設定した並列数と、`system/decomposeParDict`内で設定する`numberOfSubdomains`の値を揃えること
